timeline.label: Q1 2026 declared dividend $0.43 per share (11% increase in dividends paid in 2025); signals continued cash return after Evoqua integration
Q1 2026 revenue $2.125B (+2.7% YoY); diluted EPS $0.79 vs $0.69; four-segment platform still compounding modestly
FY2025 revenue $9.035B (+5.5%); free cash flow ~$910M; long-term debt reduced to $1.408B from $1.978B; adjusted operating margin 17.8%
Acquired Vacom Systems LLC (measurement/water quality); expands Measurement and Control Solutions footprint
FY2024 revenue $8.562B; adjusted operating income $1.373B (16.0% margin); Evoqua integration margin gains visible year two post-merger
Board and governance refresh continued post-Evoqua; integration committees wind down as segment reporting stabilizes
First full year as four-segment water platform (Water Infrastructure, Applied Water, Measurement and Control, Water Solutions and Services)
Closed Evoqua Water Technologies merger — transformed Xylem into combined equipment + outsourced water services leader (~$2.5B WSS segment at scale)
Announced Evoqua merger agreement — all-stock combination to expand treatment and services exposure
Pre-merger Xylem revenue $5.522B; three-segment structure before Evoqua combination
Spun from ITT Corporation as standalone water pure-play (historical context for segment heritage: Flygt, Goulds, Sensus brands)

industry.structure: Global water technology oligopoly with four application-focused segments; utility and industrial customers value installed base, specification lock-in, and service contracts. Post-Evoqua, Xylem competes with Veolia, Suez, Pentair, and regional pump/treatment OEMs.
industry.share_shift: Xylem gained scale in outsourced water services via Evoqua (WSS segment $2.464B FY2025); smart metering (Sensus) faces AMI upgrade cycles; municipal infrastructure spending supports Water Infrastructure transport/treatment mix.
industry.trend: Aging water infrastructure, lead-pipe replacement, PFAS treatment, and digital metering drive demand; integration synergies and restructuring ($40–55M expected 2026) continue post-merger; services and recurring revenue mix rising.
